name: AI Kubernetes workloads should not run privileged
description: AI-related Kubernetes workloads using privileged runtime settings
body:
### Security intent

This control hardens workloads by ensuring that **AI Kubernetes workloads should not run privileged**.

### Workload state reviewed

The assessment reviews AI-related Kubernetes workloads using privileged runtime settings in Kubernetes. It focuses on the workload, runtime, image, role, network, or platform setting relevant to this control.

### Remediation focus

Treat compute findings as workload changes. Confirm ownership, update infrastructure as code or deployment templates, roll out in batches where needed, and validate that new resources inherit the corrected baseline.
remediationDescription:
## Remediation

Run AI workloads with restricted pod security settings. Disable privileged mode and privilege escalation, drop unnecessary capabilities, and use read-only root filesystems where supported.

1. Identify AI workloads using privileged runtime settings.
2. Remove unnecessary host and kernel-level access.
3. Apply namespace policy, workload admission controls, and runtime monitoring for exceptions.
